description: Undergo or cause a far-reaching change in form, function, or organisation. 'moluki' is stronger than 'helui' (change), but it does not promise that every part changed or that the result improved.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips together for voiced 'm' and let the air pass through the nose into rounded middle-height back 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for stressed 'l' and continue into rounded high-back 'u'. Close the back of the tongue at the soft palate for 'k' and release into high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: A rounded nasal moves through a broad lateral centre to the crisp release of final 'ki'. So much movement within three syllables suits Phi's stronger change verb.
usage_notes: The transforming thing may be the subject, or an agent may take it as an object. 'lue' (from) and 'muo' (into) can name source and result. Extent, benefit, consent, and responsibility remain separate claims.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Solarpunk often asks whether a harmful arrangement can be reorganised rather than merely adjusted. 'moluki' makes that larger claim available without calling the result just or successful.

semantic_domains.temporal: a substantial difference established across time
